commands.review_summary:
Adds an admin-only package-name repair endpoint, response schema, and `clawhub-mod plugins repair-name` command that can dry-run, retire an occupied target name, rename the source package, optionally transfer ownership, and sync search digests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main rejects code-plugin publishes when the stored runtimeId differs from the incoming runtimeId, and the linked discussion identifies the exact OpenViking package names. The production row state still requires admin-only verification.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer/admin approval for the security-sensitive package identity repair path; no narrow automated code repair is indicated.

target.title: fix: add admin package name repair
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a guarded admin package-name repair flow for the OpenViking migration case in openclaw/clawhub#2133. The new `clawhub-mod` command performs a dry-run by default, can retire an occupied target package name, then moves the real package record into that target name and optionally transfers ownership to the target publisher.

This keeps the operation in the existing moderator/operator path: `clawhub-mod` calls an authenticated HTTP API route, and the route calls existing internal mutations that enforce admin checks and write audit logs. The repair also updates package search digests when package identity changes so public read/search state stays in sync.

## Why

The requester confirmed that they want the real package currently at `@openclaw/openviking` moved to `@openviking/openclaw-plugin`, while retiring the existing placeholder package at that target name. The desired end state is that `@openviking/openclaw-plugin` becomes the canonical package while preserving the real package record/history.

## Testing

- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `bun run ci:packages`
- `bun run ci:types-build`
- `git diff --check`

I also built `clawhub-mod` and ran the built CLI against local Convex sample rows. The local proof confirmed the dry-run plan, applied the repair, verified the final target row retained the source package id, verified the old source name no longer resolved, and verified the old target row was renamed to the dated retired name and soft-deleted.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
